human_actions_pending.context: Dev server already running on port 8000. URLs: localhost:8000/.planning/spikes/015a-restore-destructive-replace/spike.html (+ 015b + 015c). The verdict determines whether iterations need destructive restore at all (which gates the rest of the series).

decisions.decision: Iterations are conceptually distinct from baselines: baselines are immutable comparison anchors; iterations are save-states the user can rewind to
decisions.rationale: Avoided rebuilding the baseline mechanic by accident

decisions.decision: Linear history assumed for MVP — no branching/parallel iteration lines
decisions.rationale: Branching is a separate, much larger feature; not in this spike series

decisions.decision: Build all 6 spikes in risk order, 015 first because it has the highest kill risk
decisions.rationale: User picked 'All six in risk order' at the alignment checkpoint

decisions.decision: Spike 015c includes Undo via toast link with 5s window + auto-removes the auto-snapshot on undo to limit sidebar clutter
decisions.rationale: Direct response to the 'auto cards stack up' concern

next_action: Wait for user's 015 winner verdict (or hybrid description), update each 015a/b/c README Results section accordingly, then proceed to Spike 016 (snapshot scope). The winner shapes 016: if 015b (preview-only) wins, 016 changes scope to 'what's the minimum data needed to render the saved tasks as a preview.' If 015a or 015c wins, 016 stays as 'what's preserved for a true restore.'
